title: Hypertension renal risk review
source_review_basis: committed exact-section summaries and evidence records; full guideline documents were not retained for this pass

investigations.text: Lists follow-up electrolytes, renal function, lipids, urine ACR, glucose or HbA1c, liver tests, blood count, and ECG according to medication and result context.
investigations.rationale: Supports renal function, electrolytes and urine ACR review for hypertension and kidney-risk review; no decision is generated.
investigations.evidence_extract: Lists follow-up electrolytes, renal function, lipids, urine ACR, glucose or HbA1c, liver tests, blood count, and ECG according to medication and result context.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Hypertension – 27

follow_up.text: Specifies office, repeated home, and ambulatory blood-pressure assessment and hypertension staging.
follow_up.rationale: Supports blood-pressure assessment for hypertension and kidney-risk review; no decision is generated.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Specifies office, repeated home, and ambulatory blood-pressure assessment and hypertension staging.

source.title: DHA Telehealth Clinical Guidelines for Virtual Management of Hypertension – 27

follow_up.text: Supports clinician-led cardiovascular-risk discussion and review of urine, blood, retinal, and ECG assessments where actually performed or already ordered.
follow_up.rationale: Supports cardiovascular-risk and target-organ assessment for hypertension and kidney-risk review; no decision is generated.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-led cardiovascular-risk discussion and review of urine, blood, retinal, and ECG assessments where actually performed or already ordered.

source.title: Hypertension in adults: diagnosis and management

exact_section.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1-1.3.3 - cardiovascular risk and target-organ assessment

follow_up.text: Supports clinician-arranged GFR and albuminuria monitoring and evaluation of a greater-than-20-percent eGFR change or ACR doubling without mapping fixed legacy intervals.
follow_up.rationale: Supports GFR and albuminuria monitoring in CKD for hypertension and kidney-risk review; no decision is generated.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Supports clinician-arranged GFR and albuminuria monitoring and evaluation of a greater-than-20-percent eGFR change or ACR doubling without mapping fixed legacy intervals.

source.title: KDIGO 2024 Clinical Practice Guideline for the Evaluation and Management of Chronic Kidney Disease

exact_section.heading: Monitoring GFR and albuminuria in CKD
exact_section.locator: Practice Points 2.1.1–2.1.5; summary PDF pages 40 and 81–82

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Blood-pressure readings, technique, kidney history, GFR and ACR trends, medicines, adherence, target-organ findings, diagnosis, treatment and plan remain absent.
No diagnosis, risk conclusion, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for hypertension and kidney-risk review.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
